name: robot
description: CLI for running Robot Framework automation tests

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print usage instructions

name: --rpa
options.description: Turn on the generic automation mode. Mainly affects terminology so that "test" is replaced with "task" in logs and reports

name: -F
--extension
options.description: Parse only files with this extension when executing a directory

name: -N
--name
options.description: Set a name of the top level suite

name: -D
--doc
options.description: Set a documentation of the top level suite

name: -M
--metadata
options.description: Set metadata of the top level suite

name: -G
--settag
options.description: Sets given tag to all executed tests

name: -t
--test
options.description: Select tests by name or by long name containing also parent suite name like `Parent.Test`

script: for i in $(find -E . -regex ".*.robot" -type f); do cat -s $i ; done
generators.js_source: e=>{let t=e.matchAll(/(?:\*{3} ?Test Cases ?\*{3})([\S\s]*)(?:\*{3}(\w+\s?)+\*{3})*/gim),n=new Set,i=[];for(let[s,o]of t){let r=o.matchAll(/^(\w+( |-)*)+(?!.\#.*)(?!.\#.*)/gm);for(let[l]of r)l=l.trim(),l.search(/\s\s+/)==-1&&(n.has(l)||(n.add(l),i.push({name:l,description:"Test case"})))}return i}

name: --task
options.description: Alias to --test. Especially applicable with --rpa

script: for i in $(find -E . -regex ".*.robot" -type f); do cat -s $i ; done
generators.js_source: e=>{let t=e.matchAll(/(?:\*{3} ?Test Cases ?\*{3})([\S\s]*)(?:\*{3}(\w+\s?)+\*{3})*/gim),n=new Set,i=[];for(let[s,o]of t){let r=o.matchAll(/^(\w+( |-)*)+(?!.\#.*)(?!.\#.*)/gm);for(let[l]of r)l=l.trim(),l.search(/\s\s+/)==-1&&(n.has(l)||(n.add(l),i.push({name:l,description:"Test case"})))}return i}

name: -s
--suite
options.description: Select suites by name

name: -i
--include
options.description: Select test cases by tag

script: for i in $(find -E . -regex ".*.robot" -type f); do cat -s $i ; done
generators.js_source: e=>{let t=e.matchAll(/(?:^\s\s+\[Tags\])\s\s+(\w+ *)*(?!.\#.*)/gm),n=new Set,i=[];for(let[s]of t)for(let o of s.trim().substring(6).trim().split(/\s\s+/))n.has(o)||(n.add(o),i.push({name:o,description:"Tag",icon:"\u{1F3F7}"}));return i}

name: -e
--exclude
options.description: Select test cases not to run by tag

script: for i in $(find -E . -regex ".*.robot" -type f); do cat -s $i ; done
generators.js_source: e=>{let t=e.matchAll(/(?:^\s\s+\[Tags\])\s\s+(\w+ *)*(?!.\#.*)/gm),n=new Set,i=[];for(let[s]of t)for(let o of s.trim().substring(6).trim().split(/\s\s+/))n.has(o)||(n.add(o),i.push({name:o,description:"Tag",icon:"\u{1F3F7}"}));return i}

name: -R
--rerunfailed
options.description: Select failed tests from an earlier output file to be re-executed

name: -S
--rerunfailedsuites
options.description: Select failed suites from an earlier output file to be re-executed

name: --runemptysuite
options.description: Executes suite even if it contains no tests

name: --skip
options.description: Tests having given tag will be skipped

script: for i in $(find -E . -regex ".*.robot" -type f); do cat -s $i ; done
generators.js_source: e=>{let t=e.matchAll(/(?:^\s\s+\[Tags\])\s\s+(\w+ *)*(?!.\#.*)/gm),n=new Set,i=[];for(let[s]of t)for(let o of s.trim().substring(6).trim().split(/\s\s+/))n.has(o)||(n.add(o),i.push({name:o,description:"Tag",icon:"\u{1F3F7}"}));return i}

name: --skiponfailure
options.description: Tests having given tag will be skipped if they fail

script: for i in $(find -E . -regex ".*.robot" -type f); do cat -s $i ; done
generators.js_source: e=>{let t=e.matchAll(/(?:^\s\s+\[Tags\])\s\s+(\w+ *)*(?!.\#.*)/gm),n=new Set,i=[];for(let[s]of t)for(let o of s.trim().substring(6).trim().split(/\s\s+/))n.has(o)||(n.add(o),i.push({name:o,description:"Tag",icon:"\u{1F3F7}"}));return i}

name: -v
--variable
options.description: Set variables in the test data

generators.js_source: async(e,t)=>{if(!!e[e.length-1].includes(":"))return[];let{stdout:s}=await t({command:"bash",args:["-c",'for i in $(find -E . -regex ".*.(robot|resource)" -type f); do cat -s $i ; done']});return[...s.matchAll(/^\$\{(.*?)\}/gm)].map(r=>r[1]).map(r=>({name:r,description:"Variable"}))}

name: -V
--variablefile
options.description: Python or YAML file file to read variables from

name: -d
--outputdir
options.description: Where to create output files. The default is the directory where tests are run from

name: -o
--output
options.description: XML output file relative to --outputdir unless given as an absolute path. Default: output.xml

name: -l
--log
options.description: HTML log file. Can be disabled by giving a special value `NONE`. Default: log.html

name: -r
--report
options.description: HTML report file. Can be disabled with `NONE` similarly as --log. Default: report.html

name: -x
--xunit
options.description: XUnit compatible result file. Not created unless this option is specified

name: -b
--debugfile
options.description: Debug file written during execution. Not created unless this option is specified

name: -T
--timestampoutputs
options.description: Adds timestamp in a format `YYYYMMDD-hhmmss` to all generated output files between their basename and extension

name: --splitlog
options.description: Split the log file into smaller pieces that open in browsers transparently

name: --logtitle
options.description: Title for the generated log file. The default title is `<SuiteName> Log.`

name: --reporttitle
options.description: Title for the generated report file. The default title is `<SuiteName> Report`

name: --reportbackground
options.description: Background colors to use in the report file. Given in format `passed:failed:skipped` where the `:skipped` part can be omitted

name: --maxerrorlines
options.description: Maximum number of error message lines to show in report when tests fail. Default is 40, minimum is 10 and `NONE` can be used to show the full message

suggestions.name: NONE
suggestions.description: Unlimited number of lines. Shows the full message

name: --maxassignlength
options.description: Maximum number of characters to show in log when variables are assigned. Zero or negative values can be used to avoid showing assigned values at all. Default is 200

name: -L
--loglevel
options.description: Threshold level for logging

name: --suitestatlevel
options.description: How many levels to show in `Statistics by Suite` in log and report

name: --tagstatinclude
options.description: Include only matching tags in `Statistics by Tag` in log and report

script: for i in $(find -E . -regex ".*.robot" -type f); do cat -s $i ; done
generators.js_source: e=>{let t=e.matchAll(/(?:^\s\s+\[Tags\])\s\s+(\w+ *)*(?!.\#.*)/gm),n=new Set,i=[];for(let[s]of t)for(let o of s.trim().substring(6).trim().split(/\s\s+/))n.has(o)||(n.add(o),i.push({name:o,description:"Tag",icon:"\u{1F3F7}"}));return i}

name: --tagstatexclude
options.description: Exclude matching tags from `Statistics by Tag`

script: for i in $(find -E . -regex ".*.robot" -type f); do cat -s $i ; done
generators.js_source: e=>{let t=e.matchAll(/(?:^\s\s+\[Tags\])\s\s+(\w+ *)*(?!.\#.*)/gm),n=new Set,i=[];for(let[s]of t)for(let o of s.trim().substring(6).trim().split(/\s\s+/))n.has(o)||(n.add(o),i.push({name:o,description:"Tag",icon:"\u{1F3F7}"}));return i}

name: --tagstatcombine
options.description: Create combined statistics based on tags. These statistics are added into `Statistics by Tag`

script: for i in $(find -E . -regex ".*.robot" -type f); do cat -s $i ; done
generators.js_source: e=>{let t=e.matchAll(/(?:^\s\s+\[Tags\])\s\s+(\w+ *)*(?!.\#.*)/gm),n=new Set,i=[];for(let[s]of t)for(let o of s.trim().substring(6).trim().split(/\s\s+/))n.has(o)||(n.add(o),i.push({name:o,description:"Tag",icon:"\u{1F3F7}"}));return i}

name: --tagdoc
options.description: Add documentation to tags matching the given pattern

script: for i in $(find -E . -regex ".*.robot" -type f); do cat -s $i ; done
generators.js_source: e=>{let t=e.matchAll(/(?:^\s\s+\[Tags\])\s\s+(\w+ *)*(?!.\#.*)/gm),n=new Set,i=[];for(let[s]of t)for(let o of s.trim().substring(6).trim().split(/\s\s+/))n.has(o)||(n.add(o),i.push({name:o,description:"Tag",icon:"\u{1F3F7}"}));return i}

name: --tagstatlink
options.description: Add external links into `Statistics by Tag`. Pattern can use `*`, `?` and `[]` as wildcards

script: for i in $(find -E . -regex ".*.robot" -type f); do cat -s $i ; done
generators.js_source: e=>{let t=e.matchAll(/(?:^\s\s+\[Tags\])\s\s+(\w+ *)*(?!.\#.*)/gm),n=new Set,i=[];for(let[s]of t)for(let o of s.trim().substring(6).trim().split(/\s\s+/))n.has(o)||(n.add(o),i.push({name:o,description:"Tag",icon:"\u{1F3F7}"}));return i}

name: --expandkeywords
options.description: Matching keywords will be automatically expanded in the log file

name: --removekeywords
options.description: Remove keyword data from the generated log file

suggestions.name: passed
suggestions.description: Remove data only from keywords in passed test cases and suites

suggestions.name: for
suggestions.description: Remove passed iterations from for loops

suggestions.name: while
suggestions.description: Remove passed iterations from while loops

suggestions.name: wuks
suggestions.description: Remove all but the last failing keyword inside `BuiltIn.Wait Until Keyword Succeeds`

name: --flattenkeywords
options.description: Flattens matching keywords in the generated log file

name: --listener
options.description: A class for monitoring test execution. Gets notifications e.g. when tests start and end

name: --nostatusrc
options.description: Sets the return code to zero regardless of failures in test cases. Error codes are returned normally

name: --dryrun
options.description: Sets the return code to zero regardless of failures in test cases. Error codes are returned normally

name: -X
--exitonfailure
options.description: Stops test execution if any test fails

name: --exitonerror
options.description: Stops test execution if any error occurs when parsing test data, importing libraries, and so on

name: --skipteardownonexit
options.description: Causes teardowns to be skipped if test execution is stopped prematurely

name: --randomize
options.description: Randomizes the test execution order

name: --prerunmodifier
options.description: Class to programmatically modify the suite structure before execution

name: --prerebotmodifier
options.description: Class to programmatically modify the result model before creating reports and logs

name: --console
options.description: How to report execution on the console

suggestions.name: dotted
suggestions.description: Only show `.` for passed test, `s` for skipped tests, and `F` for failed tests

suggestions.name: quiet
suggestions.description: No output except for errors and warnings

name: --dotted
options.description: Shortcut for `--console dotted`

name: --quiet
options.description: Shortcut for `--console quiet`

name: -W
--consolewidth
options.description: Width of the console output. Default is 78

name: -C
--consolecolors
options.description: Use colors on console output or not

suggestions.name: auto
suggestions.description: Use colors when output not redirected (default)

suggestions.name: ansi
suggestions.description: Like `on` but use ANSI colors also on Windows

name: -K
--consolemarkers
options.description: Show markers on the console when top level keywords in a test case end

name: -P
--pythonpath
options.description: Additional locations (directories, ZIPs) where to search libraries and other extensions when they are imported

name: -A
--argumentfile
options.description: Text file to read more arguments from. Use special path `STDIN` to read contents from the standard input stream

name: --version
options.description: Print version information
